```{=html}
<div style="width:84%;margin:0 auto;text-align:left;">

 <div style="position:relative;background:rgba(157,154,224,0.1);border-left:5px solid #9D9AE0;border-radius:0 14px 14px 0;padding:1.5em 1.8em 1.6em;">
  <div style="position:absolute;top:0.4em;left:0.7em;font-family:'Georgia',serif;font-size:5em;color:#9D9AE0;line-height:0.7;opacity:0.18;user-select:none;">"</div>
  <div style="position:relative;font-size:0.92em;line-height:1.55;color:#F8F7F0;font-style:italic;font-family:'Georgia','TsangerJinKai',serif;">
   A skill is not for the <span style="color:#9D9AE0;font-style:normal;font-weight:700;">agent</span>'s benefit. The agent does not distinguish a skill from a temporary prompt: it just reads files and context. We make skills for the <span style="color:#FFB84D;font-style:normal;font-weight:700;">humans</span>, to encapsulate a workflow worth keeping.
  </div>
 </div>

</div>
```
